majorRisk: Channel dependency on Bayer/DISAGRO/UPL; revenue not publicly disclosed
shortlistRationale: #1 scorer globally (tied); Series B closed 2024 ($15M, Prosus/Viking/Seaya); world's largest soil microbiome database (55M+ taxa); 56+ country deployments; named viticulture case studies (Vinyes Domenech, Napa Valley); Fast Company #4 most innovative in agriculture 2024; dual revenue model (per-test + licensing) provides diversification

majorRisk: PE ownership complexity (Moira Capital 45.5%); extreme financial opacity; no published vineyard-specific outcomes; large-player competitive threat (Syngenta, Novonesis, BASF)
shortlistRationale: #2 scorer (tied with Biome Makers); world's largest indoor microalgae agricultural production facility in Europe; 4 EU-certified biostimulants (most of any single microalgae company); 60+ country distribution; AgroStar India partnership (Oct 2024); doubled sales 2022; €23M expansion plan underway; strong regulatory moat from certification process

majorRisk: Team of only 12; hardware-intensive model with modest funding (~$6-9M); no carbon registry methodology acceptance yet; California-only deployments; carbon market demand uncertainty
shortlistRationale: Substituted for RVA (non-profit) and Sencrop (acquired by ISAGRI Jan 2025); original #5; only commercial continuous soil carbon flux monitoring system deployed in vineyards; named premium customer roster (Duckhorn, Jordan, Joseph Phelps, Silver Oak, Boisset); UC Davis academic partnership; USDA $5M climate-smart grant; N2O sensor (2025) adds full GHG stack; highest-integrity MRV technology for vineyard carbon credits
